name: Mexicali Squares
recipeIngredient: 1 pound canned kidney beans (rinsed and drained), yielding 10 ounces
recipeInstructions:
-------------
CRUST
3/4 cup yellow cornmeal
2 teaspoons baking powder
2 egg whites or 1 Ener-G egg replacer (for 2 eggs)
1/2 cup skim milk or soy milk
2 tablespoons olive oil
1 can canned corn
Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly oil an 8-inch square baking pan or spray with nonstick cooking spray.
To prepare topping: Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add garlic, onions, and green pepper. Cook for 10 minutes, or until onions are tender and beginning to brown. Remove from heat and stir in remaining topping ingredients. Set aside.
To prepare crust: In a small bowl, combine cornmeal and baking powder, mixing well. In another bowl, combine egg whites or egg replacer (with appropriate water), milk (or soy milk), and oil. Beat with a fork or wire whisk until blended. Stir into cornmeal, mixing just until all ingredients are moistened. Stir in corn.
Spread crust mixture evenly in prepared pan. Mixture will be loose. Spoon topping evenly over crust. Bake, uncovered, for 35 minutes. Let stand 5 minutes before cutting into squares to serve.
